summary: Board of directors first discussed the possibility of exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
Board unanimously agreed to proceed with certain initial steps related to exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
Board decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives and unanimously approved engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch as financial advisor.
Board unanimously approved moving forward with contacting potential buyers, executing confidentiality agreements, and conducting meetings.
Company entered into a confidentiality agreement with New Mountain Capital.

subject_names: Zep Inc. board of directors|BofA Merrill Lynch
summary: BofA Merrill Lynch distributed a first round process letter to potential buyers requesting non-binding preliminary indications of interest due no later than April 14, 2014. Fifty potential buyers had been contacted (28 strategic, 22 financial); 25 executed confidentiality agreements.
New Mountain Capital received the marketing materials and the first round process letter but decided not to submit a preliminary indication of interest.

subject_names: 5 parties who submitted first-round indications of interest on April 14, 2014
summary: Five parties (four financial, one strategic) submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest ranging from $20.00 to $22.00 per share.

Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest ranging from $19.50 to $20.50 per share.

counterparty_names: 6 remaining bidders after Party X withdrawal (received draft merger agreement May 22)
summary: Draft merger agreement was distributed to the six remaining bidders (Party X having withdrawn).

subject_names: 6 remaining bidders after Party X withdrawal (received draft merger agreement May 22)
summary: Five of the remaining six interested parties communicated they were unable to proceed due to valuation concerns. The sixth remaining interested party declined to respond.
Board decided to terminate the process to explore strategic alternatives based on lack of buyer interest and uncertainty from the fire at the aerosol facility.
Board authorized letters waiving the 'don't ask to waive' provisions, permitting parties to make confidential proposals to acquire the Company.
New Mountain Capital contacted BofA Merrill Lynch and requested a meeting. On February 10, 2015, New Mountain Capital met with BofA Merrill Lynch and expressed interest in a potential transaction.

New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to acquire the Company for $19.25 per share, supported by a highly confident financing letter from Jefferies Finance LLC, with a request for 45-day exclusivity and a contemplated go-shop provision.

summary: New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest at $20.05 per share, indicating this was the highest price it was willing to offer. Requested 45-day exclusivity and reiterated willingness to accept a go-shop provision.

summary: Exclusivity extension signed, extending exclusivity with New Mountain Capital through April 7, 2015.

Draft merger agreement delivered to New Mountain Capital, including a 60-day go-shop provision permitting the Company to solicit alternative proposals and to terminate the merger agreement for a superior offer.
New Mountain Capital returned a revised draft of the merger agreement, reducing the go-shop period from sixty days to thirty days.

summary: New Mountain Capital reiterated that $20.05 per share was its best and final offer when approached by both Mr. Morgan and BofA Merrill Lynch about a price increase.

Board unanimously determined the merger agreement was advisable and in the best interests of stockholders, and resolved to recommend stockholder approval. BofA Merrill Lynch delivered a fairness opinion.

summary: Go-shop process commenced; BofA Merrill Lynch contacted 58 parties (strategic and financial buyers) regarding their interest in exploring a transaction. Go-shop period ran until May 7, 2015.
